summary: Matched the Target Test Game & Watch ground/stage module by replacing asm/melee/gr/grtgamewatch.s with C in src/melee/gr/grtgamewatch.c and adding a corresponding header. The PR also moved grtgamewatch.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py and switched obj_files.mk from the assembly object to the C object.

key_files.role: Deleted matched assembly implementation for grTGameWatch.
Added the matched C implementation, including StageCallbacks, StageData, initialization, gobj setup, animation callbacks, and small stub/boolean callbacks.
Added prototypes and forward includes for the grTGameWatch functions.
Changed melee/gr/grtgamewatch.c from NonMatching to Matching.
Replaced asm/melee/gr/grtgamewatch.s.o with src/melee/gr/grtgamewatch.c.o in TEXT_O_FILES.

what_changed: Added a 185-line C implementation for src/melee/gr/grtgamewatch.c.
Defined StageCallbacks grTGw_803E96F8 as three callback rows plus a NULL terminator row, preserving the original callback table layout.
Defined StageData grTGw_803E9754 with stage id 63, data file path "/GrTGw.dat", lifecycle callbacks, and flags `(1 << 0)`.

Implemented grTGameWatch_80224114 to set stage_info.unk8C.b4 false and b5 true, spawn ground IDs 0, 1, and 2 via grTGameWatch_802241B4, then call Ground_801C39C0, Ground_801C3BB4, Ground_801C4210, and Ground_801C42AC.

Implemented grTGameWatch_802241B4 as the shared ground-gobj creation/setup helper: obtains the gobj with Ground_801C14D0(id), clears Ground x8_callback and xC_callback, installs the GX link with GObj_SetupGXLink, optionally assigns callback3 to x1C_callback, optionally invokes callback0, and optionally registers callback2 with HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54.
Preserved the assembly error path in grTGameWatch_802241B4 with OSReport using the string `"%s:%d: couldn t get gobj(id=%d)\n"`, file name `"grtgamewatch.c"`, and line number 195.
Implemented animation/setup callbacks using grAnime_801C8138, Ground_801C2ED0, Ground_801C2FE0, lb_800115F4, GET_GROUND, HSD_GObjGetUserData, and gobj->hsd_obj casts to HSD_JObj.
Implemented several no-op and constant-return callbacks such as empty void functions, false-returning bool callbacks, NULL-returning grTGameWatch_802243E4, and true-returning grTGameWatch_802243EC.
Added grtgamewatch.h with include guard `_grtgamewatch_h_`, platform/baselib/dolphin/lb forward includes, and prototypes for all exported grTGameWatch symbols.
smart_moves: Modeled the assembly data section directly as typed C data: StageCallbacks for grTGw_803E96F8 and StageData for grTGw_803E9754, avoiding raw arrays while preserving layout-relevant values.

Used existing stage/ground types and helper macros, including StageCallbacks, StageData, Ground, GET_GROUND, PAD_STACK, HSD_GObjGetUserData, and HSD_JObj, instead of inventing local anonymous structs.
Kept symbol names address-based, such as grTGameWatch_802241B4, matching project convention for not-yet-semantically-named functions.
Matched the repeated ground animation initialization pattern with Ground_801C2ED0 followed by grAnime_801C8138 in both grTGameWatch_802242D8 and grTGameWatch_80224368.
Preserved subtle matching details from the assembly, including the OSReport line number constant and PAD_STACK(8) in functions that require stack-shape padding.
Updated both build selection points: configure.py marks the C file Matching and obj_files.mk links the C object instead of the asm object.
decomp_lessons: For stage modules, matching often requires translating both .text and .data: callback tables and StageData contents are as important as function bodies.
Ground creation helpers commonly index a StageCallbacks table by map/ground id and then install callback0, callback2, and callback3 into gobj/Ground state only when non-NULL.
Stage init functions may manipulate stage_info bitfields directly; here stage_info.unk8C.b4 and b5 express the original rlwimi byte writes more clearly.
Keep original assert/error-report constants when matching OSReport paths; even a source line number like 195 can be layout-relevant.
No-op functions and constant bool/NULL return functions should still be given typed prototypes in headers, because stage callback tables depend on their exact signatures.
Use PAD_STACK when a C translation is otherwise logically simple but the generated stack frame needs padding to match the original assembly.
When removing a matched asm file, update both configure.py matching status and obj_files.mk object path so the build actually consumes the new C object.
naming_conventions: File/module prefix uses `grTGameWatch_` for functions and `grTGw_` for data symbols.
Unidentified functions retain address-based suffixes, e.g. `grTGameWatch_80224114`, consistent with doldecomp placeholder naming.
Global data symbols retain original addresses in names, e.g. `grTGw_803E96F8` and `grTGw_803E9754`.
The header guard uses the project-style lowercase file name pattern `_grtgamewatch_h_`.
Callback struct members are accessed with generic names such as callback0, callback2, and callback3, reflecting existing StageCallbacks type naming rather than semantic guesses.
Ground fields use existing project names such as x8_callback, xC_callback, x1C_callback, and map_id.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Translated the deleted .data callback table into a StageCallbacks array with three populated rows and one all-NULL terminator.
Translated the deleted StageData-like data block into a typed StageData initializer, including stage id `63`, file path `"/GrTGw.dat"`, callback pointers, and flag `(1 << 0)`.
Recreated rlwimi bitfield manipulation of stage_info.unk8C as explicit C bitfield assignments `b4 = false` and `b5 = true`.
Used local temporaries in grTGameWatch_802241B4 for `HSD_GObj* gobj` and `StageCallbacks* cb = &grTGw_803E96F8[id]` to express the table-indexing pattern from the assembly.
Used HSD_GObjGetUserData and GET_GROUND where appropriate to recover Ground pointers from gobj userdata.
Inserted PAD_STACK(8) in the two functions that call Ground_801C2ED0 and grAnime_801C8138, indicating stack-layout matching pressure.
Preserved simple leaf behavior as exact no-op, false, NULL, or true return functions rather than folding them away.
follow_up_queries: Find other grt* target-test stage modules that use the same StageCallbacks and StageData layout as grtgamewatch.
Search for PAD_STACK(8) in ground/stage callbacks that call Ground_801C2ED0 and grAnime_801C8138.
Compare grTGameWatch_802241B4 with other stage gobj setup helpers using Ground_801C14D0, GObj_SetupGXLink, callback0, callback2, and callback3.
Look for other PRs that translate stage_info.unk8C bitfield writes from rlwimi assembly into C bitfields.
Search for OSReport strings of the form `couldn t get gobj(id=%d)` to reuse matching line-number and file-name tactics.
